# CHAOS Todo HTML View Contract

Human-digest views generated by `chaos:todo` are **static HTML**, not Markdown. Durable
Markdown item files remain the only source of truth. HTML views are always regenerable from
`.chaos/todo/items/*.md` + `.chaos/todo/index.md` and must never be hand-edited.

## Generated files

```text
.chaos/todo/todo-report-YYYY-MM-DD.html   # dated snapshot digest for the run that produced it
.chaos/todo/views/index.html              # current-state dashboard (all items)
.chaos/todo/views/public-alpha.html       # target: public-alpha
.chaos/todo/views/v1.html                 # target: v1
.chaos/todo/views/open-blockers.html      # status: open|in-progress|blocked|needs-decision, priority: BLOCKER
.chaos/todo/views/by-type.html            # grouped by type
.chaos/todo/views/by-change.html          # grouped by relatedChanges, when useful (optional)
```

Only regenerate a view when its source item files changed, or when `--refresh` is passed
explicitly (`policies.todo.htmlViews.regenerateOnlyWhenSourcesChange`).

## Hard requirements

- Static: no server, no build step to view it — open the `.html` file directly in a browser.
- Self-contained: **all** CSS inline in a `<style>` block in the document `<head>`. No external
  stylesheet link, no external/CDN JS, no remote images/fonts.
- No JavaScript. Use plain anchors (`<a href="#id">`) and native `<details>`/`<summary>` for any
  collapsible/filterable UX — never `<script>`.
- Every title, path, snippet, owner, and evidence excerpt interpolated into the HTML must be
  HTML-escaped (`&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`). Never interpolate raw Markdown/user text unescaped.
- No secrets or sensitive content — the same redaction discipline as every other CHAOS report.
- Lightweight: prefer plain tables/lists over heavy markup; keep the file reviewable in a PR
  diff.
- Include the generated-artifact comment immediately after `<!DOCTYPE html>`:

  ```html
  <!-- Generated by chaos:todo. Do not edit manually. Durable todo items live under .chaos/todo/items/. -->
  ```

## Required visual structure (every generated view)

1. `<title>` — e.g. "CHAOS Todo — Public Alpha".
2. Generated metadata line: generated timestamp (ISO-8601), source command (`chaos:todo
   --refresh` or whichever invocation produced it), repository context summary (provider,
   branch, confidence) when resolved.
3. Dashboard cards (counts): open items, blockers, public-alpha blockers, v1 blockers, stale
   items, needs-decision items.
4. Recommended next actions (short ordered list, top 3-5).
5. Table of open items (id, title, priority, target, type, owner, links to the source
   Markdown item file and, when resolvable as a relative link, the source evidence artifact).
6. Grouped sections by target: Public alpha, v1, vNext, Later.
7. Duplicate candidates section (pairs/groups flagged during the last dedup pass, with
   disposition if already resolved).
8. Stale items section (items flagged `stale`, with the missing-evidence reason).
9. Source coverage section (which configured sources were scanned, counts found per source).
10. Warnings / confidence gaps section (LOW-confidence items, missing config, inferred
    defaults used).
11. Footer: "Generated artifact — not source of truth. Source of truth: `.chaos/todo/items/`.
    Regenerate with `chaos:todo --refresh`."

Use the literal skeleton in `todo-html-template.md` as the base for every generated view;
populate its placeholders per view (`index.html` shows everything, `public-alpha.html` /
`v1.html` filter to that target, `open-blockers.html` filters to open blockers,
`by-type.html` groups by type instead of target).

## Escaping helper (conceptual)

When generating HTML content (whether via a script or directly), escape every interpolated
string with the standard five-entity HTML escape (`&amp;`, `&lt;`, `&gt;`, `&quot;`, `&#39;`)
before inserting it into any attribute or text node. Do this for titles, paths, owners,
snippets, and evidence excerpts — no exceptions.
